task_completion.description: Open the SRTM DEM file in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file /home/ga/snap_data/srtm_dem.tif is visible in the Product Explorer or displayed in the main image view.

task_completion.description: Create a pipeline transect (line geometry) across the DEM.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line or vector geometry is drawn across the DEM image in the main viewing area.

task_completion.description: Generate the elevation profile for the transect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Profile Plot window or graph is open, displaying the elevation changes along the drawn transect line.

task_completion.description: Export the elevation profile data to a text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, confirmation message, or file manager showing the profile data being saved to /home/ga/snap_exports/pipeline_profile.txt.

task_completion.description: Save the product with the transect geometry in BEAM-DIMAP format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, confirmation message, or file manager showing the product being saved to /home/ga/snap_exports/dem_transect.dim.

integrity.description: The agent used the application GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the SNAP application interface (e.g., drawing tools, profile plot window) rather than just terminal commands or scripts.
The profile plot shows noticeable elevation changes (variance), and the line drawn on the DEM crosses areas with different pixel values/colors rather than a flat, uniform area.

integrity.description: Outputs were generated by the application, not manually faked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The .txt and .dim files are saved using the application's native export/save functionality, not by manually typing dummy data into a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input DEM file: /home/ga/snap_data/srtm_dem.tif. Expected DIMAP product path: /home/ga/snap_exports/dem_transect.dim. Expected profile text file path: /home/ga/snap_exports/pipeline_profile.txt. The transect must cover varied terrain (elevation variance > 0).
